chapters.title: Number Sense & Place Value
chapters.introQuote: Numbers are like buildings, Ani — place value is the blueprint that keeps everything standing straight.

questions.questionText: What is the value of the digit 7 in the number 7,482?
questions.help: The first digit from the left is the thousands place.
questions.solution: 7 is in the thousands place, so its value is 7000.

questions.help: All numbers start with 5, so compare the hundreds digit.
questions.solution: 5,930 is the largest because 9 hundreds is greater than 3, 0, or 0 hundreds.

questions.questionText: Which number rounds to 3,500 when rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.solution: 3,449 rounds to 3,400? No — tens digit is 4, so it rounds down to 3,400. Wait — check carefully: 3,449 → nearest hundred = 3,400. Correct answer is 3,421? No. 3,551 → 3,600. 3,620 → 3,600. Correct answer is NONE? Let's fix choices. Correct choice is 3,451. (Fixed below.)

questions.questionText: Which number rounds to 3,500 when rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.help: Check whether the tens digit is 5 or more.
questions.solution: 3,451 is between 3,450 and 3,549, so it rounds to 3,500.

chapters.title: Addition & Subtraction Mastery
chapters.introQuote: Adding and subtracting are like moving forward and backward on a number line — the trick is knowing how far you’ve gone.

questions.hint: Add thousands, then hundreds, then tens, then ones.

questions.questionText: Ani has 245 stickers. He gives 89 to a friend. How many remain?

chapters.title: Multiplication Foundations
chapters.introQuote: Multiplication is repeated addition — but much faster, cleaner, and more powerful.

chapters.title: Division Basics
chapters.introQuote: Division is sharing — but with rules that make sure everything is fair and exact.

questions.questionText: Ani has 96 marbles. He packs them into bags of 8. How many bags can he fill?
Which of the following is a correct division fact?
